_meta.description: Curated, downloadable data sources for the platform's CLIMADA-based analyses — population/exposure, hazard layers, forecast feeds, building footprints, and calibration losses. Surfaced in the Data tab so a user can fetch what a capability needs. Sources tagged fetch.mode='auto' can be downloaded + refined into a CLIMADA-ready hazard from the UI ('Fetch & ingest'); the rest link out (login-gated, operational feeds, or large drop-ins).
_meta.provenance: URLs + access terms adversarially verified 2026-06-23. Access: 'free' = no login; 'login'/'registration' = free account; 'paid'/'academic' flagged for commercial use. Auto-ingest is scoped to the current portfolio's region.

categories.label: Modeled exposure (LitPop)
categories.note: GPW is the one file CLIMADA LitPop cannot auto-download — get it, unzip the .tif into ~/climada/data, then 'Model exposure' on the Map works. WorldPop / GHSL are no-login drop-ins.

categories.label: Hazard layers
categories.note: 'Fetch & ingest' downloads + refines the source into a CLIMADA-ready hazard scoped to your portfolio's region and writes it to the local catalog below — runs then use it automatically (offline / reproducible).

categories.label: Forecast feeds
categories.note: Operational forecasting needs climada_petals; ECMWF Open Data is the only feed TCForecast reads natively. These are live feeds, not one-time downloads.

sources.name: GPW v4.11 Population Count (2020, 30 arc-sec)
sources.notes: GeoTIFF: gpw_v4_population_count_rev11_2020_30_sec.tif. GPW v5 not yet released — use v4.11. Durable entry: earthdata.nasa.gov catalog. Login-gated, so cannot be auto-fetched.

sources.name: WorldPop 1 km population (no login)
sources.notes: Per-country 1 km population GeoTIFF (login-free) → ~/climada/data for LitPop from_raster.

sources.name: Natural Earth 110m country boundaries
sources.notes: Small open GeoJSON; downloaded via the Data tab's fetch tool.

sources.name: GHSL GHS-POP R2023A (EU JRC)
sources.notes: Some products in Mollweide (EPSG:54009) — reproject to 4326.

sources.name: TCTracks — generate TC hazard from IBTrACS
sources.for: Generate your own TC wind hazard (IBTrACS historical + synthetic perturbation)
sources.scenarios: present-day synthetic tracks (apply Knutson scaling for futures)
sources.notes: Downloads IBTrACS, perturbs tracks, builds a TropCyclone wind hazard over your portfolio bbox → local catalog. Resource-intensive; needs network.

sources.name: TCRain — generate TC rainfall hazard (R-CLIPER)
sources.for: Generate a physical TC rainfall hazard (climada_petals TCRain, R-CLIPER) — replaces the indicative tc_rain ramp
sources.scenarios: present-day synthetic tracks (apply Knutson scaling for futures)
sources.notes: Same IBTrACS track pipeline as TCTracks, then the analytic R-CLIPER rainfall model (mm) over your portfolio bbox → local catalog. Then select the tc_rain peril and Run. Resource-intensive; needs network.

sources.name: CLIMADA Data API — Tropical cyclone (synthetic)
sources.for: Tropical cyclone (cache locally for offline runs)
sources.notes: Cached as country-coverage synthetic-track windspeed hazard keyed to your portfolio's country.

sources.name: CLIMADA Data API — River flood (ISIMIP)
sources.for: River flood (cache locally for offline runs)
sources.notes: ISIMIP-derived flood-depth hazard; cached for your portfolio's country.

sources.name: WRI Aqueduct Floods (riverine GeoTIFFs)
sources.for: River flood (real depth maps, refined to a CLIMADA hazard)
sources.notes: Return-period inunriver_* GeoTIFFs read over /vsicurl for your portfolio's bbox only (no multi-GB download); each RP becomes one event.

sources.name: WRI Aqueduct Floods (coastal / sea-level-rise GeoTIFFs)
sources.for: Coastal flood / SLR (real inundation maps, refined to a CLIMADA hazard)
sources.notes: Return-period inuncoast_* GeoTIFFs read over /vsicurl for your portfolio's bbox; each RP becomes one event (incremental exceedance frequency). Then select the coastal_flood peril and Run.

sources.name: CLIMADA Data API — Wildfire (historical)
sources.notes: Country-coverage MODIS-derived brightness-temperature (WFseason) hazard; single-country portfolios only. Filed under historical/2020 to match the wildfire runner.

sources.name: CLIMADA Data API — Earthquake (observed)
sources.notes: Country-coverage observed MMI hazard; single-country portfolios only. Filed under observed/2020 to match the earthquake runner.

sources.name: Copernicus DEM GLO-30 (AWS)
sources.notes: GLO-30 COG tiles mosaicked over your portfolio's bbox via /vsicurl (no login); written to the local catalog so TC surge uses it automatically.

sources.name: SPEI Global Drought Monitor (CSIC)
sources.notes: ODbL share-alike — matters for redistributing derived layers.

sources.name: ECMWF Open Data (ensemble TC tracks)
sources.notes: Tracks only when a TC is active. Client: pypi.org/project/ecmwf-opendata.

sources.name: FEMA OpenFEMA NFIP Redacted Claims (US flood)
sources.notes: API access was recently suspended — re-check availability.

sources.name: EM-DAT international disaster losses
sources.notes: Commercial use needs a paid UCLouvain license.
